flashcards.question: What is the mean of the following dataset: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25?
In a skewed distribution, which measure of central tendency is most resistant to outliers?
What is the mode of the dataset: 3, 7, 7, 9, 11, 7, 15?
What is the relationship between variance and standard deviation?
flashcards.correct_answer: Standard deviation is the square root of variance
incorrect_answers: Variance is the square root of standard deviation
Variance equals standard deviation squared times n

flashcards.question: If a dataset has a variance of 64, what is its standard deviation?
What is the probability of drawing an Ace from a standard deck of 52 cards?
If two events are independent, how do you calculate the probability of both occurring?
incorrect_answers: Take the average of both probabilities

flashcards.question: What is the null hypothesis (H₀) in statistical testing?
flashcards.correct_answer: A statement of no effect or no difference that is tested
incorrect_answers: The research hypothesis we want to prove
The alternative to the research hypothesis
The conclusion of the statistical test

flashcards.question: What does a p-value of 0.03 indicate when using α = 0.05?
incorrect_answers: Fail to reject the null hypothesis

flashcards.question: What is a Type I error in hypothesis testing?
incorrect_answers: Failing to reject a false null hypothesis

flashcards.question: What is a Type II error in hypothesis testing?
flashcards.correct_answer: Failing to reject a false null hypothesis

flashcards.question: What does a 95% confidence interval mean?
flashcards.correct_answer: If we repeated the sampling many times, 95% of intervals would contain the true population parameter
incorrect_answers: There is a 95% probability the true parameter is in this specific interval
95% of the data falls within this interval

flashcards.question: What happens to the width of a confidence interval when the sample size increases?
What is the range of values for a Pearson correlation coefficient?
What does a correlation coefficient of -0.85 indicate?
In simple linear regression, what does the slope coefficient represent?
flashcards.correct_answer: The change in the dependent variable for a one-unit change in the independent variable
incorrect_answers: The value of Y when X equals zero
The correlation between X and Y
The average value of the dependent variable

flashcards.question: What percentage of data falls within ±2 standard deviations of the mean in a normal distribution?
What does the Central Limit Theorem state?
flashcards.correct_answer: The sampling distribution of the mean approaches normal as sample size increases, regardless of population distribution
incorrect_answers: Sample means always equal population means
Large samples eliminate all sampling error

flashcards.question: When should you use a t-test instead of a z-test?
flashcards.correct_answer: When the population standard deviation is unknown and sample size is small
incorrect_answers: When the population standard deviation is known
When sample size is greater than 100
When testing proportions instead of means

flashcards.question: What is the primary difference between a one-tailed and two-tailed test?
flashcards.correct_answer: A one-tailed test examines effect in one direction; a two-tailed test examines both directions
incorrect_answers: A one-tailed test uses half the sample size
A two-tailed test is always more powerful
